description.text: Once possessed by Fax Celestis XVI, the Eater of Beatings has now chosen you as its next hero. It desires the blood of diggles for sustenance. Do you dare defy the Hat God? It is studded with elk bone and the blood of diggles.

Rather than pointy hats or other headwear traditionally associated with wizardry, Dadinavian wizards wear these sporty, aerodynamic sunglasses. This is the most basic version, usually gifted to an apprentice graduating into full wizardry by their mentor.

Dadinavian wizard castes follow a complicated, color-based protocol for determining who is a better wizard. Red wizards are several steps above blue (except in Eastern Dadinavia, for some reason), and these red shades are more powerful to match.

Dadinavian green wizards are among the higher castes (surpassed only by orange wizards, white wizards, black wizards, and The High Prismatic Wizard). This green-tinted pair of sunglasses has seen better days, but they're more than functional.
Orange wizard shades are worn by all the uppermost castes of Dadinavian wizards. Why? Tradition states that 'white sunglasses look stupid' and 'black sunglasses make it impossible to see anything'.
A candle in a hand-held candlestick holder. Maybe you can use this to light up your life a little.
Inscribed on the handle is the phrase 'Please Return to P. Revere'.
This whimsical handheld lantern is shaped like a traditional jack-o-lantern. The light emitting from its mouth gives an eerie glow to your surroundings.

This magical hurricane lantern does not have a flame or similar light source inside the glass (though it does still emit light). Instead, when you are struck with a spell, the lantern absorbs some of the incoming energy and generates a mild antimagic bubble around you as a defense mechanism.
This terrifying handheld lantern is carved like a traditional jack-o-lantern. You could swear that the face changes when you're not looking at it.
Every little bit counts.

item.name: The Seventy Maxims of Maximally Effective Mercenaries

description.text: Maxim 6: If violence wasn't your last resort, you failed to resort to enough of it.

item.name: History of the World, Part I

description.text: Far more boring than the movie version.
A treatise on the best way to defeat your enemies, see them driven before you, and to hear the lamentation of their women. Parts of it appear to be bloodstained.
Previously owned by three scantily clad witches.

item.name: History of the World, Part II

description.text: Someone's torn out half the pages and scrawled all over the others in some infernal language.
The pictures in here could take your breath away.
Seems to have a little bit on everything.
Traditional Dadinavian wizard attire is incomplete without this flirtatious apron. What makes it flirtatious? Well, typically one does not wear anything underneath it.

Dadinavian wizards who ascend to the red caste are given this ceremonial attire as part of their commencement. They are typically given practical magical enchantments that grant the wearer resistances against the elements of common magical mishaps. Why it's down here is beyond anyone's knowledge.

Dadinavian wizards who ascend to the highest caste (black) wear this traditional garb. Each one is unique and enchanted differently, though they do have some common features. One thing they do have in common, however, is that they're not very good at stopping traditional physical attacks. Try not to get hit with this on: the black of the apron will cover up bloodstains, but will do nothing to prolong your lifespan.

Apprentice wizards devised the original cargo shorts to carry additional magical components, as their robes did not have pockets. However, it was not long before they were coopted as the national garb of Dadinavia. This pair has an enchantment that spontaneously creates components when struck with significant kinetic energy.

item.name: Cargo Shorts of the Purple Wizard

description.text: Upon leaving their apprenticeship, Dadinavian wizards change their robes (and associated cargo shorts) from iron gray to funky purple, a color chosen because it covers alchemical mishaps very easily. Like the apprentice version, these journeyman cargo shorts carry a similar kinetic enchantment, spontaneously creating crafting materials when struck with sufficient force.

item.name: Cargo Shorts of the White Wizard

description.text: Dadinavian master wizards wear this traditional garb. They function similarly to an apprentice's, but are far more stylish in their white color (and far more powerfully enchanted). Bonus: they are enchanted with a self-cleaning cantrip, so the dust, dirt, grime, blood, ichor, and other detritus of the dungeon will not damage your fabulous shorts.

These adventuring jeans are a slim cut and are a little worn in the knees, but are otherwise serviceable. Adventuring jeans differ from traditional jeans primarily in that adventurers wear them (rather than the common folk).

These adventuring jeans are a slim cut and are a little worn in the knees, but are otherwise serviceable. Adventuring jeans differ from traditional jeans primarily in that adventurers wear them (rather than the common folk).

These adventuring jeans are a slim cut and are a little worn in the knees, but are otherwise serviceable. Adventuring jeans differ from traditional jeans primarily in that adventurers wear them (rather than the common folk).

These adventuring jeans are a slim cut and are a little worn in the knees, but are otherwise serviceable. Adventuring jeans differ from traditional jeans primarily in that adventurers wear them (rather than the common folk).
These red boxing gloves make it very difficult to perform precise movements, but they're also pretty excellent for beating up monsters.
While the enchantment on these frost-covered gloves isn't enough to actually freeze anything, it is definitely uncomfortable for anyone on the receiving end of their assault.
You're not sure why these gloves smell like a fresh fruit salad, but they do.
Downside: wearing these is immeasurably bad for your health. Upside: they are just as bad for your enemies' health.

This belt has a shiny silver star for a buckle. Every protagonist needs a notable belt these days. Some even wear multiple belts. Like that lady who has a skirt made entirely from belts. That's a little extreme, but maybe you can start with this one and see where it goes.
This belt has a large golden star for a buckle. You'd say it feels lucky, but luck isn't a stat that you know of. At least its shiny!

Originally, scabbards were a way to protect your sword while it wasn't in use. Dwarves weren't satisfied with this, however, and developed a scabbard with a tiny whetstone right at the opening, so that every time you drew your sword it would sharpen it anew automatically. This is one of those scabbards, though it has seen better days.
This scabbard is an improvement over the scabbard of keen edges, having a much more finely tuned sharpening mechanism inside of it. It has seen some use, but is still in remarkably good shape.
The pinnacle of dwarven auto-sharpening-scabbard design.
This unassuming quiver always seems to have bolts in it. Occasionally they just fall out.
Only a Dadinavian wizard can pull off socks with sandals. These hideous foot coverings are magically enchanted to interact with leylines and increase your magical prowess.
Boasting an even stronger connection to the leylines hidden in the earth, these garish sandals (and the associated socks) impart greater magical prowess and quicker mana restoration to their wearer.
Green Dadinavian wizards wear these traditional sandals and their associated socks. Try not to think too hard about the last foot that was in these, or you'll be as green as the sandals.

Orange Dadinavian wizards are one of the highest castes of wizard in the highly-stratified Dadinavian society, but even strong wizards can succumb to the horrors of these dungeons. Hopefully you have better luck than they did.
Only the High Prismatic Wizard is higher than the Black Wizards of Dadinavia. How did this pair of powerful sandals (and associated socks) end up down here?
These tan sneakers are perfect for lawnmowing, grilling, and talking about baseball on hot summer days.
The pinnacle of Dadinavian wizard fashion, these Dreds come in a classic blue and are paired with tall white socks.
Dadinavian red wizards, one of the intermediate castes, wear these stylish trainers while discussing politics and cryptocurrency.
These shoes have absolutely nothing to do with cowboys.
These enchanted trainers are perfectly balanced for quick, light movements and minor feats of daring.
This limited-run pair of sneakers has inverted colors for the soles and logo. Sneakerheads go gaga over these, so of course you're going to use them to kick monsters' faces in, aren't you?
...can you even wear this on a different day?
For the stylish gentleman on the go!
The pictures in this locket have long since faded, but the sentiment is still there. Wear this in memory of those who've gone before you into these depths.
This diggle-shaped charm gives you a strong urge to dig.
This simple silver moon pendant has been enchanted with general-purpose enhancement magic.

Legends are spoken of phylacteries like this one being used to store the souls of liches after they undergo their dark transformation. You're not sure if this is the phylactery of Dredmor or of another lich, but the lights that play about inside the crystal are unsettling enough that you'd rather not find out.

Legends are spoken of phylacteries like this one being used to store the souls of liches after they undergo their dark transformation. You're not sure if this is the phylactery of Dredmor or of another lich, but the lights that play about inside the crystal are unsettling enough that you'd rather not find out.

Legends are spoken of phylacteries like this one being used to store the souls of liches after they undergo their dark transformation. You're not sure if this is the phylactery of Dredmor or of another lich, but the lights that play about inside the crystal are unsettling enough that you'd rather not find out.

Legends are spoken of phylacteries like this one being used to store the souls of liches after they undergo their dark transformation. You're not sure if this is the phylactery of Dredmor or of another lich, but the lights that play about inside the crystal are unsettling enough that you'd rather not find out.
This phylactery isn't quite like the others... Strange. Is it...yours?
It would be fairly noble to return this lost engagement ring to its rightful owner, but there's a fairly good chance they're already down here somewhere.
The ultimate in square-shaped finger ornamentation, the martyr's ring is designed for those who stick their necks (and other extremities) out for others at their own expense.
Instead of a gemstone, this ring appears to have a fully functional (and strangely alive) eye set into it. It looks remarkably like some of the eyes you've seen peeping out of the dungeon walls.
A particularly enterprising (or lazy) spellsword has enchanted this ring so that it will automatically shuffle you through channeling modes when striking enemies. Give it a whirl!
Imbued by a powerful meteorologist, this ring makes vague predictions that are sometimes accurate.
This ring is difficult to keep pictured in your mind. Are you even wearing a ring?

It is a widely known fact that an individual can only wear one ring on each hand, otherwise magical energies conflict with each other and create a resonance cascade resulting in the collapse of the local space-time-magical confluence, utterly destroying the hapless idiot who tried to put on too many rings. Lord Dredmor, being a powerful lich, knows this, and so he has cast aside this, his Third Ring, as he has crafted two others that are superior to this one.
The mighty Excalibur is now in your hands!
A Hammer of Righteousness is a powerful tool in the hands of an Inquisitor. It's pretty good in plain hands too.
The Hammer Consortium, a subsidiary of the Bolt Council, brings to you the Hammer of Extra Righteousness, for when your enemies need extra smiting.
The Hammer Consortium's Double Righteous model does triple the damage of the baseline Hammer of Righteousness!

The Hammer Consortium's latest model, the Hammer of Extreme Righteousness, is the perfect tool for the Inquisitor on the go! Rend your enemies' brains from their skulls with fewer swings than even our Hammer of Double Righteousness!

Here at the Hammer Consortium, we asked ourselves: is the Hammer of Extreme Righteousness extreme enough? And we had to say no. Say hello to your new best friend, the Hammer of Incredible Righteousness!

The Hammer Consortium's laboratory technicians were hard at work hammering out the finer points of this model for years. We're not sure just how they jammed so much righteousness into one hammer, but they did it!

The Hammer of Unending Righteousness is the ultimate in fiend-slaying design. The Hammer Consortium has spared no expense: this model is lovingly handcrafted by Hammer Consortium artisans out of rare-earth cold iron, anointed with bespoke holy oils, and finished with the finest ironwood haft money can buy. It also includes a bonus cupholder!

You thought the Hammer Consortium was done with the Hammer of Unending Righteousness, didn't you? Well think again, because the Hammer of Exemplary Righteousness has all the features of the Hammer of Unending Righteousness, but also includes a certificate of authenticity and limited-run identification paperwork. It's almost too nice to smash into the faces of your fiendish enemies! ...almost.

The Hammer Consortium lost this hammer aeons ago in a beaurocratic mixup, and they've been trying to relocate it since. Last you heard, it was in the hands of an insurance company. What is it doing here?
This cane is better for prodding traps than beating monsters into submission, but beggars can't be choosers.
This stylish canesword is perfect for the cad on the go!

The shillelagh is a traditional weapon consisting of a staff with a heavy spherical weight on the end. They share more in common with a mace than with a wizard's staff, but this one will make your magic (and your critical hits!) stronger all the same.

Orcs are not known for their magical prowess, nor for their attention to detail. They are, however, known for bashing in the heads of their enemies with extreme prejudice. You're not sure what exactly this 'wand' is going to do next, but it does seem like an otherwise serviceable staff.
The Staphylococcus is named for the disease it inflicts upon enemies. Try not to get any on you.

This quite literal music staff is shaped like a treble clef. It hums harmoniously along with the sounds of the dungeon, and its tune seems to wend its way into cracks in the walls, weakening their structure and revealing hidden passages.

This staff radiates heat (but in a warm-and-fuzzy sort of way (not a burning-to-death sort of way (though it will burn your enemies to death if you hit them enough (but sometimes it will cause your enemies to change allegiances before then (if you hit them hard enough))))).
Also known as a 'punching dagger', the katar is a favorite of demon-hunting assassins.
This cobalt-infused steel katar has a wonderfully sharp edge that is simply begging to be inserted into the nearest enemy.
Don't ask what bluesteel is. We're not sure either, but it does make some particularly nice weaponry.
Flametouched metals retain some of the heat of the forge, allowing the wielder to impart some of that heat directly to their enemies.
So named for the emerald-colored liquid that weeps from the gem in this katar's hilt, emerald katars are highly toxic and generally not conducive to continued life.
Reminiscent of wolverines for some reason, these claws are made of flametouched iron and are extremely hot.
You're fairly sure that the blade of this katar is cut from glacial ice and formed into its current shape. It's so cold that it's chilling the air around you.

First mined by dwarves from their deepest mines, orichalcum is an almost sacred substance that is infused with base elemental energies. This claw, somehow both hot and cold, sparks rhythmically with electricity.

Sai are extremely effective in the hands of a master and can be used in a variety of ways. Unfortunately, you have not spent your formative years mastering this implement of death, so you'll just have to do your best and hope it all works out in the end.
Elves are well-known amongst the dwarven community for their sneakiness and their archery skills. This knife is evidence to the former.

targetHitEffectBuff.name: Staked by the Stake of Staking

description.text: A stake of staking is best used against the undead, or when the stakes are high and the steaks are rare.
This sawtooth-bladed dagger is designed to catch (and break!) the swords (and other dangerous extremities!) of your enemies (those guys who are trying to kill you!).

Dwarven war chisels are a rare artifact from days of yore. It is rumored that these tools were repurposed from the dwarves' workshops out of necessity and then were refined to their current state with years upon years of bureaucratically-impeded research and development. Are they perfect? No. Did you file form 305B with the 5335X Addendum (Type 4)? If you had, it would be.
The bohemian earspoon uses STAR POWER to inflict gnarly death upon its victims.
A natural evolution of the bohemian earspoon, the bohemian earspork is a star-powered polearm that comes with its own theme music (a pretty catchy tune about someone named Galileo...).

Unlike most polearms, the lucerne hammer is essentially a sledge on the end of a very long stick. Forget about 'given a long enough lever I could move the world': given a long enough hammer, you could crush even the most daunting of enemies. It can be fairly difficult to wield properly, but when it hits, it hits HARD.
This battered farming implement has seen better days.

A scythe is, like most other weapons, essentially a sharp blade on the end of a stick. Thousands of years of development in metallurgy and weaponsmithing, and it still boils down to how big the blade is and what direction it points in.
Scythes are the preferred weapon of farmers and necromancers everywhere.

Dipping ordinary steel into the Lake of Lost Souls transforms it into blacksteel, a powerful metal charged with necromantic energies. Weapons crafted from it are rare, but powerful. Whoever made this scythe really wanted to lean into the edgy necromancer vibe.
May or may not have a matching masque.
THE NEGASCYTHE IS AN IMPLEMENT OF LIGHT AND DARK, DESIGNED TO BOTH REAP AND SOW. IT IS CRAFTED FROM THE COALESCED VAPOR OF NUANCE. IT THIRSTS FOR SOULS. DARE YOU DENY THE NEGASCYTHE ITS HUNGER?
These cheap plastic knuckles barely fit over your fingers. The holes are square too. Man, these are really crappy.
Traditionally held by bouncers, mobsters, and overzealous pugilists, these knuckles have now fallen into your hands. Ha, a hands pun. The narrator will be here all week. Try the veal.
Iron, being a harder metal than brass, is better suited to bashing faces in. Why not give your enemies a hand with their defensive training? Another hand pun! Those never get old.
Cold steel in hand is worth two in your enemy. ...wait.
These charred iron knuckles have been infused with flaming elemental energies that you can evoke by mashing them into an enemy.
These blessed silver-plated knuckles are infused with holy energies that destroy the enemies of righteousness. You've got to hand it to the smith, this is pretty fine work. Oh my, more hand puns.
These gold-plated knuckles are inordinately heavy, but they hit very hard and make you feel like the Hand of the King. These hand puns never get old.

These totally righteous knuckles are infused with gnarly, awesome energies. Call them forth by reaching out and brofisting your enemies: if they perish, then they were not righteous enough for the Almighty Bro.
Looks like a knock-off.
There's no substitute for the real thing!
Holding this bow makes your hands cold. Makes your enemies cold too.
Glimmers powerfully in your hands. Almost blinding, actually.
Gives off a gentle, calming sensation.
This bow whispers 'blooooood' softly in your ear when you wield it. It seems...thirsty.

This dangerous contraption of dwarven make reeks of desperation against terrifying odds. It has a large magazine of bolts attached to the top, and cranking the lever sends these bolts flying in all directions.
Nothing matters...more than ending the lives of the monsters in this dungeon! This confusing crossbow makes you care and not care at the same time.
Best used when you can't risk missing.

item.name: Linh Quang Kim Trao Than No

description.text: Also known as The Saintly Crossbow of the Supernaturally Luminous Golden Claw, this impressive repeating crossbow is manufactured from the claw of a giant golden turtle. It is incredibly heavy and has a very large magazine.
Bring an entire spectrum of hurt to your enemies.
This potion seems especially volatile.
This shimmery red liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells failtly of fertilizer and growing plants.
This shimmery blue liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of ice and mint.
This shimmery cobalt liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of ozone.
This shimmery bronze liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of charcoal.
This shimmery green liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of alkaline.
This shimmery blue liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of crisp snow.
This shimmery emerald liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of an incoming storm.
This shimmery ruby liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of blood.
This shimmery midnight liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of tree sap.
This shimmery rose-colored liquid seems to attune itself to you. It smells faintly of old books and new binders.
This flask of liquid quickly shifts colors all aross the spectrum, and bubbles so vigorously the vial shakes in your hand.
This boiling liquid smokes darkly in the vial.
This vial is frozen solid.
This vial flashes and rumbles, as if a small storm were brewing under the stopper.
This vial is filled with a clear liquid. The vial itself seems sturdier than others of similar manufacture.
This disgusting goo stinks with a palpable miasma.
This tightly-stoppered bottle is filled with a nebulous liquid reminiscent of the milky way.
I have always looked upon alchemy in natural philosophy to be like enthusiasm in divinity, and to have troubled the world much to the same purpose.
And lo, did the Alchemist speak unto the people: 'Duck!'
This dark and brooding liquid fizzes loudly. It smells of grave dirt.
This tightly stoppered bottle doesn't seem to contain anything. Odd.
Amateur alchemists try to turn this into gold. Real alchemists turn this into something they drink.
Tiny swords glitter as they swirl through the liquid in this bottle.
Tiny shields glitter as they swirl through the liquid in this bottle.
The label on this is practically unintelligible. You think a 'brostorative' is a good thing, but who knows, bro?

The Arcane Wizards of Dadinavia first designed this unique wizarding implement a long time ago. Wizard spatulas have seen a resurgence in recent years, particularly among the middle-aged father demographic of wizards.
This industrial-strength dungeon cleaner claims that it kills 99.9% of diggles!
You know what they say about small wands...
This wand burns hotly in your hand, promising cleansing fire and pyrrhic rebirth.
That's what she called it, anyway. (Gets stronger the more potential peons are in your immediate vicinity.)
This dark rod quietly whispers 'booodiiiiiieeees'. The power of this effect increases when you stand atop your defeated enemies, the more the better.
This page details a recipe. Hopefully it's something you can use.
Just as it says on the tin, lifeleech traps make it very difficult to continue living.
Just as it says on the tin, lifeleech traps make it very difficult to continue living.
Just as it says on the tin, lifeleech traps make it very difficult to continue living.
Just as it says on the tin, lifeleech traps make it very difficult to continue living.
Just as it says on the tin, lifeleech traps make it very difficult to continue living.
Eldritch energies swirl about, congealing wherever the arcane eye on the wall sets its gaze.
Eldritch energies swirl about, congealing wherever the arcane eye on the wall sets its gaze.
Eldritch energies swirl about, congealing wherever the arcane eye on the wall sets its gaze.
Eldritch energies swirl about, congealing wherever the arcane eye on the wall sets its gaze.
Eldritch energies swirl about, congealing wherever the arcane eye on the wall sets its gaze.
The highly judgemental trap stares at you with a level, judging gaze. If you had a mother-in-law, this would be her eye staring out of the wall, not some freakish arcane monstrosity.
The eye gazing at you from the wall fixes a steady, disapproving gaze on you, more powerful than any other gaze you have ever felt. For some reason, you feel guilty about what you did last night.
Sphinxes are notorious peeping toms, a hobby they practice by peering out of cracks in dungeon walls. Watch out, though, they shoot lasers when noticed.
Juvenile sphinxes are no exception to the peeping-tom rule of sphinx habits. In fact, they are some of the worst offenders.
Not above their voyeuristic heritage and childhoods, the only thing improved about adult sphinxes is their aim.
This trap is more mature than to go into premature/mature jokes. Also, that was like ONE time, seriously.
Old sphinxes gotta get their jollies from somewhere.
This glaring eye stares out of a crack in the wall at you, blinking slowly, daring you to cross into its path.
This glaring eye stares out of a crack in the wall at you, blinking slowly, daring you to cross into its path.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel stupid just looking at it.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel really stupid just looking at it.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel very stupid just looking at it.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel vastly stupid just looking at it.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel incredibly stupid just looking at it.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel inordinately stupid just looking at it.
This glowing purple field of runes makes you feel excruciatingly stupid just looking at it.
no can think good blinky purple trap thing make dumdum
This trap looks like it's load-bearing.
This trap looks like it's load-bearing.
This trap looks like it's load-bearing.
This trap looks like it's load-bearing.
This trap looks like it's load-bearing.
Only those actually reading this text are safe from the fury of Grogthulu!
Lord Dredmor has an aggressive policy against vermin in his dungeon. It's unclear if you fall into that category.
This trap makes it very clear that Lord Dredmor considers you a rodent to be exterminated.

Originally used by the dwarves in the Great Footy Incursion in 312 BD, the dwarves quickly discovered that these jaw-like traps are useful on far more than just footies. Unfortunately, they also discovered that they work equally well on dwarves.
The Great Footy Incursion had thousands of casualties, including the dwarves' standards.
Despite the necessity for thousands upon thousands of traps, some craftsdwaves maintained a higher standard for their creations. This is not one of those creations.
Despite the necessity for thousands upon thousands of traps, some craftsdwaves maintained a higher standard for their creations. Unfortunately for you, this is one of them.

These footy traps were originally sown around the gates of heavily defensible fortifications during the Great Footy Incursion of 312 BD. Since they're still here, you can only assume one of two things: either they worked remarkably well, or they didn't work at all.
Holy crap, some lunatic hooked this footy trap up to a battery! Madness!

Close inspection of this footy trap quickly reveals that the teeth of this devious device have been slathered with a vile toxin. There is also a warning label: 'keep out of children do not injest'. Language was not a dwarven strong suit, it seems.

This particularly heinous contraption not only will clamp down on a footy's (or anything else's) foot to wreak wanton destruction, but will also inject the hapless fool with a variety of toxins and venoms. Don't be one of those fools.
